channel.title: Whamcloud Community JIRA
channel.description: This file is an XML representation of an issue

item.title: [LU-19841] LBUG - mdt_fix_reply()) ASSERTION( md_size > md_packed )
item.description: This ASSERTION should be replaced to return a EINVAL since the invalid value could be caused by some unstable network environment.

status.description: The issue is open and ready for the assignee to start work on it.

comments.comment:
"Oleg Drokin <green@whamcloud.com>" merged in patch https://review.whamcloud.com/c/fs/lustre-release/+/63820/
Subject: LU-19841 mdt: reset md_packed after buffer shrink
Project: fs/lustre-release
Branch: master
Current Patch Set:
Commit: 599cbfd8507dded5b6904cd79389b03e515722a7

"Alex Zhuravlev <bzzz@whamcloud.com>" uploaded a new patch: https://review.whamcloud.com/c/fs/lustre-release/+/63820
Subject: LU-19841 mdt: reset md_packed after buffer shrink
Project: fs/lustre-release
Branch: master
Current Patch Set: 1
Commit: e390456683581b9576d5bdc8879b424cad06de79

I'm able to reproduce using this test:

diff --git a/lustre/include/obd_support.h b/lustre/include/obd_support.h
index 86dce48611..a5f16b15ed 100644
--- a/lustre/include/obd_support.h
+++ b/lustre/include/obd_support.h
@@ -777,6 +777,7 @@ extern bool obd_enable_fname_encoding;
 #define OBD_FAIL_MDS_PAUSE_CREATE_AFTER_LOOKUP 0x2401
 #define OBD_FAIL_MDS_CONNECT_ACCESS            0x2402
 #define OBD_FAIL_MDS_PAUSE_GETATTR             0x2403
+#define OBD_FAIL_MDS_PAUSE_BIG_LOVEA           0x2404
 
 /* PLEASE, KEEP NUMBERS UP TO 0x3000 RESERVED FOR OBD_FAIL_MDS_* */
 
diff --git a/lustre/mdt/mdt_handler.c b/lustre/mdt/mdt_handler.c
index 952525461c..6a4a1b396f 100644
--- a/lustre/mdt/mdt_handler.c
+++ b/lustre/mdt/mdt_handler.c
@@ -1030,6 +1030,7 @@ int mdt_big_xattr_get(struct mdt_thread_info *info, struct mdt_object *o,
 
        info->mti_buf.lb_buf = big_lmm;
        info->mti_buf.lb_len = big_size;
+       CFS_FAIL_TIMEOUT(OBD_FAIL_MDS_PAUSE_BIG_LOVEA, cfs_fail_val);
        rc = mo_xattr_get(env, mdt_object_child(o), &info->mti_buf, name);
 out:
        if (using_big_lmv) {
diff --git a/lustre/mdt/mdt_open.c b/lustre/mdt/mdt_open.c
index 10a35d07f0..0484d58ed6 100644
--- a/lustre/mdt/mdt_open.c
+++ b/lustre/mdt/mdt_open.c
@@ -1097,6 +1097,9 @@ static int mdt_refetch_lovea(struct mdt_thread_info *info,
 
        if ((ibits & MDS_INODELOCK_LAYOUT) == 0)
                return 0;
+       /* XXX: simulate no layout lock due to conflict */
+       return 0;
+
        if (!S_ISREG(lu_object_attr(&o->mot_obj)))
                return 0;
 
diff --git a/lustre/tests/sanity.sh b/lustre/tests/sanity.sh
index aaa8266873..4944295482 100755
--- a/lustre/tests/sanity.sh
+++ b/lustre/tests/sanity.sh
@@ -36302,6 +36302,24 @@ test_910()
 }
 run_test 910 "Test the erasure_coding module"
 
+test_1000() {
+       local tf=$DIR/$tfile
+       mkdir -p $MOUNT2 && mount_client $MOUNT2
+       $LFS setstripe -c -1 $tf || error "can't touch"
+       $LFS mirror extend -N4 -c -1 $tf || error "can't mirror"
+#define OBD_FAIL_MDS_PAUSE_BIG_LOVEA           0x2404
+       do_facet mds1 "$LCTL set_param fail_val=3 fail_loc=0x80002404"
+       $MULTIOP $MOUNT2/$tfile oc &
+       local PID=$!
+       sleep 0.3
+       $LFS mirror split --mirror-id 1 -d $tf || error "can't rm mirror 1"
+       $LFS mirror split --mirror-id 2 -d $tf || error "can't rm mirror 1"
+       $LFS mirror split --mirror-id 3 -d $tf || error "can't rm mirror 1"
+       $LFS mirror split --mirror-id 4 -d $tf || error "can't rm mirror 1"
+       kill -USR1 $PID
+       wait
+}
+run_test 1000 "hoho"
 
 complete_test $SECONDS
 [ -f $EXT2_DEV ] && rm $EXT2_DEV || true

the result:

[   82.622447] LustreError: 17479:0:(mdt_lib.c:987:mdt_fix_reply()) ASSERTION( md_size > md_packed ) failed: 
[   82.622770] LustreError: 17479:0:(mdt_lib.c:987:mdt_fix_reply()) LBUG
[   82.622963] CPU: 0 PID: 17479 Comm: mdt00_000 Tainted: G        W  O      -------  ---  5.14.0 #6
[   82.623191] Hardware name: QEMU Standard PC (i440FX + PIIX, 1996), BIOS 1.17.0-8.fc42 06/10/2025
[   82.623435] Call Trace:
[   82.623519]  <TASK>
[   82.623597]  dump_stack_lvl+0x57/0x7d
[   82.623698]  lbug_with_loc.cold+0x5/0x51 [libcfs]
[   82.623831]  mdt_fix_reply+0x68e/0x760 [mdt]
[   82.624064]  mdt_reint_internal+0x530/0x11f0 [mdt]
[   82.624212]  mdt_intent_open+0x132/0x420 [mdt]
[   82.624388]  ? mdt_intent_fixup_resent+0x210/0x210 [mdt]
[   82.624536]  mdt_intent_policy+0x245/0x1180 [mdt]
[   82.624687]  ldlm_lock_enqueue+0x41e/0x8b0 [ptlrpc]
[   82.624882]  ? _raw_read_unlock+0x1a/0x30
[   82.624981]  ldlm_handle_enqueue+0x31a/0x16b0 [ptlrpc]
[   82.625169]  tgt_enqueue+0x5b/0x230 [ptlrpc]
[   82.625366]  tgt_request_handle+0x51b/0x1d30 [ptlrpc]
[   82.625567]  ptlrpc_main+0xfcb/0x2ae0 [ptlrpc]
[   82.625748]  ? ptlrpc_watchdog_init+0xb0/0xb0 [ptlrpc]
[   82.625979]  kthread+0xce/0xf0

I added a simple check;

@@ -1095,8 +1095,13 @@ static int mdt_refetch_lovea(struct mdt_thread_info *info,
        struct mdt_body *repbody;
        int rc;
 
-       if ((ibits & MDS_INODELOCK_LAYOUT) == 0)
+       if ((ibits & MDS_INODELOCK_LAYOUT) == 0) {
+               LASSERTF(info->mti_big_lov_used == 0,
+                       "%s %s\n",
+                       S_ISREG(lu_object_attr(&o->mot_obj)) ? "reg" : "nonreg",
+                       (ma->ma_valid & MA_LOV) ? "LOVEA" : "");
                return 0;
+       }

and I can hit the assertion quite easily:

 LustreError: 6820:0:(mdt_open.c:1099:mdt_refetch_lovea()) ASSERTION( info->mti_big_lov_used == 0 ) failed: reg LOVEA

literally this means that we may have LOVEA fetched into a big buffer without layout lock, i.e. there was another thread modifying LOV EA (e.g. shrinking it)

also, I find the following confusing:

if (info->mti_big_lmm_used) {	
        /* big_lmm buffer may be used even without packing the result
         * into reply, just for internal server needs */
        if (req_capsule_has_field(pill, &RMF_MDT_MD, RCL_SERVER))
		md_packed = req_capsule_get_size(pill, &RMF_MDT_MD, RCL_SERVER);

so mti_big_lmm_used=1 doesn't mean we really need to return LOVEA, and the following lines confirm that:

 /* now we need to pack right LOV/LMV EA */
lmm = req_capsule_server_get(pill, &RMF_MDT_MD);
if (info->mti_attr.ma_valid & MA_LOV) {
        memcpy(lmm, info->mti_attr.ma_lmm,
		info->mti_attr.ma_lmm_size);

thus it could be that we didn't even need to pack LOVEA into the reply, but still hit LASSERT()
one reason to skip LOVEA is a race with a mirror creation/removal when we can't get LAYOUT lock, but we still fetch LOVEA before lock enqueue.

I have finished a analysis in DDN-5403. You can take a look for detail.{quote}

thanks, I did. and now this is even more confusing IMO.

I've got two questions:

1) md_size is actual LOV EA size, md_packed is size of reply buffer (LOVEA part), if md_size < md_packed, then why a big buffer was used?

2) what's wrong with sending back a small LOV EA using a larger buffer? why do we need to return -EINVAL leading to something like EIO to app potentially?
I have finished a analysis in DDN-5403. You can take a look for detail.

So do you mean we need add more data verification on client too? {quote}

I mean that we don't understand what happened, what the values were and why they are considered wrong.

this problem has been reported many times already, in different tickets - not a random issue.

at least we need to analyze the crash dumps.
Hi, Alex, So do you mean we need add more data verification on client too? Maybe some generic way to verify package directly is more effective, like CRC?

I find it unlikely that we hit exactly the same corrupted field (and no other) in RPC many times in few hours like happened to the system.

rather this is some disagreement on the client side about number of active/configured OSTs leading to a small buffer to receive large LOV EA.
Is it possible to test fault injection into the packet stream to see if this is the reason for the assertion, and as Alex mentioned how the client deals with EINVAL?
hmm, then what exactly will happen when this error is returned to the client?

Hi, Alex, It is instance from customer:

regarding all the occurrences of this bug, we find a relation between the crashes and the state of an infiniband switch and operation on this infiniband switch .

So the root cause is not lustre. No more crash since the bad switch has been repaired

So just conclude the value could be corrupted while network transfer.
can you please clarify how this happens?

"Yang Sheng <ys@whamcloud.com>" uploaded a new patch: https://review.whamcloud.com/c/fs/lustre-release/+/63664
Subject: LU-19841 mdt: check md_size value
Project: fs/lustre-release
Branch: master
Current Patch Set: 1
Commit: fb4d1ac9f2627eacf999f2103a9f59470e467508
